Shelby Eliasek

Director of Operations

2nd Year at UVA: Class of 2028

Hometown: Charlotte, North Carolina

Majors: Public Policy and Leadership (Frank Batten School); Political and Social Thought Studio

Minor: Studio Art

Research Interests: Constitutional Law, Separation of Powers

About Shelby

Shelby Eliasek is from Charlotte, North Carolina. She is majoring in Public Policy and Leadership in the Frank Batten School at the University of Virginia with a double major in Political and Social Thought and a Studio Art minor. Her areas of interest include constitutional law, social justice, and legal policy.

At UVA, Shelby continues to hone her public speaking and research skills on the UVA Undergraduate Moot Court team. During her first season (2024-25), Shelby was the D.C Regional Moot Court Tournament quarter-finalist, and she advanced to compete at the University of Houston Nationals Preliminary Round Tournament, held at the University of Houston Law Center. Through Moot Court, she learned how to write case outlines, read and summarize court decisions effectively, and collaborate with her fellow teammates. She currently serves as Moot Court's Assistant Treasurer for the 2025-26 season.

In addition to Moot Court at UVA, Shelby pursues her love for reading, writing, and law through her work with the Undergraduate Journal of Law and Politics. As a founding member of the Journal, Shelby helped build the program, recruited writers and editors, and established a stable vision for the club. She served as the Director of Engagement during the Journal's first year, working diligently to spread word about the club to any interested undergraduates. Now, Shelby is the Director of Operations, and she works personally with writers and editors to maintain the Journal's publication schedule, to communicate important updates, and to organize club events. She also runs the Journal's social media pages and aided in the initial creative design for the website.

Shelby published her first piece in the Journal in January of 2026. Her research is an examination of cases on public school policies regarding the use of gender non-conforming names and pronouns for students, and the rights of parents to be notified of these changes. When taking a break from writing, Shelby is an avid painter, runner, and reader.

Publications

"Schoolhouse Secrets: Parental Rights and Gender Identity Disclosure in the American Classroom"

Education Law • January 2026

An examination of cases on public school policies regarding the use of gender non-conforming names and pronouns for students, and the rights of parents to be notified of these changes.

Contact

Email: vua7tx@virginia.edu

LinkedIn: Shelby Eliasek
